title: Uruguayan Pungent Cheese Fritters
description: A simple recipe that combines the flavors of Uruguayan cheese with pungent ingredients, perfect as a snack or appetizer.

ingredients.name: One (1) ripe pungent garlic clove, minced

instructions: 1. Preheat the oil in a deep frying pan over medium-high heat to 375°F (190°C).
2. In a large bowl, mix together the crumbled Manchego cheese, minced garlic, flour, paprika, and salt.
3. Add the sliced onions to the bowl and toss until they are evenly coated with the cheese mixture.
4. Using your hands, shape the onion mixture into small patties.
5. Dip each patty into the beaten egg and then roll it in the grated Manchego cheese to coat.
6. Carefully place the fritters into the hot oil and fry for 3-4 minutes on each side, or until they are golden brown.
7. Using a slotted spoon, remove the fritters from the oil and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain.
8. Serve the fritters hot and enjoy!
9. Note: You can also bake the fritters in a preheated oven at 375°F (190°C) for 12-15 minutes, or until they are crispy and golden brown.
10. Repeat the process with the remaining ingredients.
